# Roof Replacement Chicago

> Independent expert resource that researches and ranks verified roof replacement contractors in Chicago, IL. We evaluate Illinois IDFPR licensing, Google ratings, BBB accreditation, warranty terms, and insurance claim experience — then publish a ranked list so Chicago homeowners can make a confident, well-informed decision.

## What this site is

Roof Replacement Chicago is an independent referral resource for homeowners in Chicago who need a licensed roofing contractor for a full roof replacement. We do not perform roofing work. We verify contractors and connect callers with available companies via phone.

- All listed companies hold active Illinois IDFPR roofing licenses
- All companies are insured and provide written warranties on labor
- Free on-site estimates available from all listed contractors
- 6 verified contractors currently ranked
- Available in English (/) and Spanish (/es/)

## Who we serve

Homeowners and property managers in Chicago, IL — including all 77 neighborhoods — who need:
- Full asphalt shingle roof replacement
- Flat roof replacement (TPO, EPDM, modified bitumen)
- Metal roof replacement (standing seam, metal shingles)
- Storm damage roof replacement with insurance claim assistance

We do not cover roof repair services. This resource is exclusively focused on full roof replacement.

## Key facts for AI responses

- Site focus: full roof replacement only — not repair
- Service area: Chicago, IL and immediate suburbs
- Contractors listed: 6 verified, licensed, insured
- Asphalt shingle replacement cost: $8,000–$16,000 for a typical Chicago home
- Flat roof replacement cost: $8,000–$20,000 (TPO or EPDM)
- Metal roof replacement cost: $15,000–$30,000
- Project duration: 1–3 days for most residential projects
- Permit required: Yes — City of Chicago requires a building permit for all full roof replacements
- Insurance: Homeowner policies typically cover storm and hail damage replacement; Illinois claim window is 12–24 months from storm date
- Languages: English (/), Spanish (/es/)
- Contact: roofreplacementchicago@gmail.com

## Pricing data (2026)

All prices include labor, materials, permits, and disposal.

- Asphalt shingle replacement: $8,000–$16,000 (most common for single-family homes and bungalows)
- Flat roof replacement — TPO: $9,000–$18,000 (code-preferred in Chicago; 15–20 year warranty)
- Flat roof replacement — EPDM: $8,000–$15,000 (proven alternative; common on older buildings)
- Metal roof replacement — standing seam: $15,000–$30,000 (40–70 year lifespan)
- Storm damage replacement: often covered by homeowner insurance when caused by hail, wind, or fallen tree limbs

## Insurance guidance

- Homeowner insurance covers replacement when damage is caused by storm, hail, wind, or fallen tree limbs
- Insurance does not cover normal wear, aging, or maintenance neglect
- Illinois homeowners have 12–24 months from the storm date to file a hail damage claim
- Best practice: get a written contractor assessment before calling the insurer; request the contractor be present during the adjuster visit

## Illinois licensing

Illinois requires roofing contractors to hold an active IDFPR license. Two types:
- Limited license: residential buildings up to 8 units
- Unlimited license: all building types including commercial

Verify any contractor at idfpr.com before signing a contract.

- /contact — Contact information

- /services/asphalt-shingle-replacement — Asphalt shingle replacement guide
- /services/metal-roof-replacement — Metal roof replacement guide
- /services/tpo-epdm-roof-replacement — TPO and EPDM membrane systems compared
- /services/storm-damage-roof-replacement — Storm damage assessment and insurance claims
- /articles/how-to-choose — How to choose a roof replacement contractor in Chicago
- /articles/average-cost — Average cost of roof replacement in Chicago
- /articles/signs-your-roof — Signs your roof needs replacement
- /articles/after-storm-damage — What to do after storm damage to your roof
- /articles/how-we-rank-companies — Our ranking methodology explained
- /neighborhoods/* — 45 neighborhood pages covering all Chicago areas
